pages.text: Fuel Ports hold $(l)liquid fuel$() — bucket it in or pipe it. Ports stacked vertically merge into one tank per fluid.$(br2)- $(l)Lava$() burns toward 1,650°C$(br)- $(l)Molten Blaze$() reaches 3,500°C for the hottest melts
pages.title: Feeding the Fire

pages.text: The forge heats toward its fuel's target and cools when starved. Every material has a melting point — $(l)iron needs 1,538°C$(), so a fresh lava forge warms up for a moment before iron starts to drip.$(br2)Hotter forges melt *faster*, not just hotter. Blaze fuel pays for itself.
pages.title: Temperature Matters
